It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Charlwoodia ensata include limonoids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Charlwoodia ensata is typically used in traditional herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
